key_insight: A lightweight transfer-learning plus hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász optimization makes BiSeNetV2 far more robust to reflective weld seams without changing model size or speed.
engineering_relevance: This improves real-time seam perception for construction welding robots, helping maintain reliable automation under harsh lighting and specular metal surfaces.
method: Start with a BiSeNetV2 backbone and apply transfer learning for seam segmentation.
Use a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss to stabilize learning on thin, difficult seam regions.
Target reflection robustness through optimization rather than adding architectural complexity.
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while keeping FLOPs, parameters, and inference speed unchanged.
watch_item: Results are promising, but deployment may still depend on how well the model generalizes to new reflective materials, weld geometries, and site-specific lighting conditions.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
